title: 10 best herbal essential oils for toothache
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for toothache are natural remedies extracted from plants that provide relief from toothache pain due to their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and analgesic properties. The best herbal essential oils for toothache include Eucalyptus globulus, Zingiber officinale, Mentha x piperita, Syzygium aromaticum, Cymbopogon citratus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Cinnamomum zeylanicum, Cinnamomum verum, Hyptis suaveolens, and Salvia officinalis. If left untreated, toothache can cause immense pain, discomfort, and difficulty in eating, sleeping, and concentrating, ultimately affecting daily life and productivity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with toothache because of its antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria and fungi, which are common causes of tooth infections. When applied topically to the affected area or added to a toothpaste, tea tree oil can help reduce inflammation and kill germs that may be causing the pain. Additionally, its analgesic properties provide instant relief from toothache discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing sore teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: Tea Tree Essential Oil has antibacterial properties that can help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to a toothache.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, making it a potential natural remedy for toothache relief.
Antiviral: Tea Tree Essential Oil's antiviral properties can help combat viral infections that may be causing toothache symptoms, such as dental abscesses or gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve toothache pain.
Leaves: The leaves contain antimicrobial compounds that can combat infection-causing bacteria in the mouth, reducing inflammation and alleviating toothache symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melaleuca alternifolia leaves from a trusted supplier ensuring at least 1 liter of fresh material.
Steam distill the harvested leaves using a clevenger apparatus with distilled water and a condenser for 4 hours.
Collect the resulting essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities taking approximately 30 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature away from direct sunlight taking no longer than 2 weeks.

Use the extracted melaleuca alternifolia essential oil as needed for toothache relief by mixing a few drops with water and swishing it in the mouth for up to 3 times daily for a maximum of 1 week duration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, it can help numb the pain caused by a toothache, providing quick relief from discomfort. The oil's antibacterial properties also aid in combating underlying infections that may be contributing to the toothache, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in the affected area. Additionally, its soothing effects can ease stress and anxiety associated with dental pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eucalyptol's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth and gums, providing relief from toothache symptoms.

Antibacterial: Eucalyptol has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain oral bacteria, which can help prevent infection and promote healing in areas affected by tooth decay or gum disease.
Analgesic: Eucalyptol has analgesic properties, which can help numb the pain associated with toothache, providing temporary relief until proper dental treatment is sought.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum inflammation and reduce pain associated with toothaches.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain flavonoids and terpenes that have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to prevent infection and reduce swelling in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus leaves in the early morning after dew has evaporated for maximum oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or low-temperature drying methods within 24 hours of collection.
Crush dried eucalyptus leaves into a coarse powder using mortar and pestle to release essential oils.
Steam distill the powdered eucalyptus with water at a temperature of 100°c for 2-3 hours to extract oil.
Filter the resulting oil through cheesecloth or coffee filters to remove any impurities and yield high-quality eucalyptus globulus essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with toothache because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The oil's antibacterial compounds also help combat the bacteria that cause tooth decay and gum disease, promoting oral health. Additionally, thyme's analgesic qualities numb the affected area, providing quick relief from toothache discomfort. By applying a small amount of thyme essential oil to the affected tooth or gum, individuals can experience fast and effective pain relief without relying on harsh chemicals.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Thyme essential oil's antibacterial property helps to combat the bacterial infections that can cause toothaches, reducing inflammation and killing bacteria that may be contributing to pain.
Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory property reduces swelling and pain associated with toothaches, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.
Anesthetic property: Thyme essential oil's anesthetic property helps to numb the affected area, temporarily relieving pain and discomfort caused by a toothache.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used for their analgesic and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with toothaches.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain high amounts of menthol, which helps to numb the gum and reduce sensitivity caused by toothaches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the plant material using a clean cloth or paper towels for at least 2 hours.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris plant material using a still with 30 grams of plant material and 500ml water.
Collect the essential oil that condenses in the receiving flask after 1 hour of distillation.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities or sediment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, it reduces pain and swelling, providing relief from the discomfort caused by a toothache. Additionally, lavender oil's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the toothache. As a result, English lavender essential oils can provide fast and effective relief from toothaches, making it a natural and soothing remedy for dental health issues.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial: English lavender essential oil has antibacterial properties, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the mouth, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with toothaches.
Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums and surrounding tissues, providing relief from toothache symptoms.
Sedative: English lavender essential oil has a calming and sedative effect, which can help relax the jaw and facial muscles, reducing tension and discomfort caused by toothaches.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant are rich in calcium and magnesium, which help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with toothaches.
Flowers: Lavender flowers contain a high concentration of linalool, an ester that has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves and flowers at dawn.
Dry the harvested material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 14 days.
Place 10 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia into a glass jar filled with 100 milliliters of high-quality carrier oil.
Steep the mixture for 21 hours at room temperature, shaking occasionally, before straining and discarding solids.
Filter the resulting essential oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, then seal and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with toothache because its cooling and analgesic properties can provide instant relief from pain and inflammation. The oil's ability to numb the affected area can help reduce sensitivity, making it easier to eat, drink, and even sleep while experiencing discomfort. Additionally, peppermint oil's antibacterial properties can also help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the toothache, promoting a healthy and pain-free mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint essential oil's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with toothaches, providing relief from discomfort.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of peppermint essential oil helps to numb the area affected by the toothache, reducing sensitivity and pain.

Antimicrobial: Peppermint essential oil's antimicrobial property helps to combat bacteria and other microorganisms that may be contributing to the toothache, promoting healthy oral flora and preventing infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing high amounts of menthol and other compounds, peppermint roots are valued for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing toothaches.
Leaves: The leaves of the peppermint plant are rich in menthone, a natural anesthetic that can help numb the pain of a toothache, providing relief from discomfort and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or store-bought ones in sufficient quantity for distillation process.
Wash the harvested peppermint leaves gently with distilled water to remove any dirt and debris thoroughly.
Load the cleaned peppermint leaves into a stainless steel distillation apparatus and add distilled water at a ratio of 1:3.
Steam distill the peppermint leaves mixture for approximately 30-40 minutes or until the oil yield reaches 0.5% to 1.2%.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil through a paper coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with toothache because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains a compound called calendulic acid, which has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth, providing relief from toothaches. Additionally, pot marigold essential oils have antibacterial properties that can help combat underlying infections causing tooth decay or gum disease, addressing the root cause of the toothache. By applying this oil topically to the affected area, it provides a natural solution for temporary pain relief and promotes oral health.

Anti-inflammatory: The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to alleviate pain and discomfort caused by toothaches, reducing swelling and redness in the affected area.
Anesthetic: Pot marigold oil has mild anesthetic properties, which can help numb the area around the toothache, providing temporary relief from sharp pains and aches.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are used to create an oil that reduces swelling and pain associated with toothaches.
Seeds: Abundant in fatty acids, seeds produce a soothing oil that cools the affected area and provides temporary relief from toothache pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers on a sunny day when they are fully open.
Dry the flowers thoroughly in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried flowers into small pieces and transfer them to a glass jar.
Steam distill the chopped flowers using a distillation apparatus at a pressure of 0.5 bar for 1 hour.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel into a clean glass vial.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sesame essential oils helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, sesame oil's active compounds, such as sesamin and sesamolin, work to numb the pain and reduce swelling around the tooth. remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Sesame oil's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacteria that cause tooth decay and gum disease, while its anti-inflammatory effects can reduce swelling and pain in the gums.

Antioxidant properties: Sesame oil's high antioxidant content can help protect the gums and teeth from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to periodontal disease and tooth decay.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesamin and sesamolin, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate tooth pain.
Seeds: Containing sesamol, a natural antibacterial agent that can combat tooth infections and gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sesamum indicum seeds from mature plants in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested seeds in a well-ventilated area for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 15 minutes.
Mix the powdered sesamum indicum with carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks under sunlight and shake occasionally before filtering and storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves for 24 hours in a warm dark place without direct sunlight.
Steam distill the dried leaves using 20 grams of leaves per liter of distilled water.
Collect the essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth within 1 hour.
Store the extracted oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning and dry them completely within 24 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof ethanol and let it steep for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hyssop essential oils helps with toothache because of its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried hyssopus officinalis leaves in sufficient quantity for distillation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat toothache with herbal essential oils is by mixing peppermint oil with clove oil. The antibacterial properties of tea tree oil can also help combat infection, while eucalyptus oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling. supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to toothache that are treated with herbal essential oils are various oral health issues and pain conditions. Lavender oil soothes sore gums and reduces anxiety, while clove oil relieves tooth sensitivity and gum pain. These herbal essential oils can be applied topically or incorporated into oral care products for effective relief.

remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with toothache by reducing inflammation and pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest mature eucalyptus globulus leaves by cutting them from the plant at dawn or late afternoon.
Steam distill 20 kg of the freshly harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves for 3 hours to extract the oil.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separatory funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the collected oil through a filter paper to remove any impurities and debris within 15 minutes.
Store the final eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with toothache because it has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. Curcumenol: Helps with toothache by its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the gums and teeth, and also exhibit antimicrobial properties to prevent infection.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used because the rhizomes of ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate toothache pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes when they are mature and before flowering.
Steam distill the harvested rhizomes for 3 hours in a distillation apparatus at 100°c.
Collect the resulting distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove impurities and contaminants.
Store the final herbal zingiber officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 5°c.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The menthol and menthone content in the leaves of Peppermint makes it effective for numbing toothache pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated for maximum oil quality.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a 2:1 ratio of leaves to water for 3-4 hours to release oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or by decantation to obtain 20-30 ml of oil.
Store the extracted oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid to prevent oxidation for up to 6 months.

Steam distill the dried flowers in a still with 5 liters of water at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the resulting oil using a condenser and filter it through a paper filter to remove impurities.
Store the obtained syzygium aromaticum essential oil in a glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dark place.

Distill the separated essential oil a second time to remove any remaining water content and impurities slowly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum essential oils helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinnamomum zeylanicum bark, cut into small pieces, and dry it in a well-ventilated area for at least 2 weeks.
Weigh 500g of dried cinnamomum zeylanicum bark and transfer it to a glass container for extraction.
Add 2 liters of ethanol to the container, seal it, and let it steep in a dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Filter the mixture using a buchner funnel and filter paper to separate the liquid from the solids, discard the solids.
Distill the liquid under vacuum using a rotary evaporator to obtain the cinnamon oil.

Rhyzomes: Cinnamomum verum rhyzomes essential oil is used to relieve toothache pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyptis suaveolens essential oils helps with toothache because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It contains compounds like bornyl acetate and bornyl ester, which have analgesic and anesthetic effects, providing relief from toothache pain. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help to reduce inflammation and fight off infection, promoting healing and recovery. This natural remedy has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to alleviate toothache symptoms, making it a popular choice for those seeking a natural alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: Helps with toothache by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, reducing pain and inflammation in the affected area.
α-Pinene: Contributes to relieving toothache by its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the gums and teeth.
Cineole: Assists in alleviating toothache by its analgesic and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce pain and prevent infection in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in bioactive compounds, making them a primary source for toothache relief essential oils.
Flowers: They contain essential oils that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate toothache pain.
Stems: They also contain bioactive compounds that are used in toothache relief essential oils, providing additional pain relief and antibacterial benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh hyptis suaveolens leaves and flowers from mature plants for optimal oil production.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to separate the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a condenser at 40-50°c for 30 minutes.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities for 10-15 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight-fitting lid for long-term preservation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils helps with toothache because of its natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oils contain compounds like thujone and camphor that work together to numb the affected area, providing quick relief from toothache pain. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Salvia officinalis essential oils help to reduce bacterial infections in the mouth, which can contribute to toothaches. This makes it a popular natural remedy for oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Trichlorogenic acid: It helps with toothache by reducing inflammation and relieving pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Carnosic acid: It helps with toothache by reducing swelling and alleviating pain due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Bornyl acetate: It helps with toothache by numbing the affected area and providing relief from pain due to its analgesic and local anesthetic properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part to make essential oils for toothache due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: Roots are another key part used to make essential oils for toothache due to their analgesic and antiseptic properties.
Buds: Buds are also used to make essential oils for toothache due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce pain and inflammation in the mouth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature salvia officinalis leaves and flowers for distillation when they are in full bloom.
Clean the harvested leaves and flowers thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Load the cleaned leaves and flowers into a distillation apparatus at a ratio of 1:5.
Steam distill the loaded apparatus for 2-3 hours at a temperature of 100-150 degrees fahrenheit.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil and discard the remaining plant material.
